nodes.context: African American street gang originating in Chicago, Illinois.
Predecessor organization formed in 1958 that later became the Black Disciples.
Rival gang led by Larry Hoover that merged with Black Disciples in 1969.
Unified gang created by merger of Black Disciples and Gangster Disciples in 1969.
One of two rival factions after Barksdale's death in 1974.

edges.evidence_quote: In 1958, a group of young teenagers from the Hyde Park , Englewood , and Kenwood areas of Chicago formed an organization known as the "Devil's Disciples".
edges.context: Devil's Disciples was the predecessor organization that evolved into Black Disciples.

edges.evidence_quote: In 1969, Larry Hoover , the leader of the rival gang Gangster Disciples , agreed to a merger with Barksdale to create a unified gang called the "Black Gangster Disciples Nation."
edges.context: Black Disciples merged with Gangster Disciples in 1969 to form Black Gangster Disciples Nation.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Gangster Disciples ( conflict ) , Vice Lords , Black P. Stones , Mickey Cobras
edges.context: Infobox lists Gangster Disciples as a rival.

edges.evidence_quote: In 1969, Larry Hoover , the leader of the rival gang Gangster Disciples , agreed to a merger with Barksdale to create a unified gang called the "Black Gangster Disciples Nation."
edges.context: Black Disciples was a constituent part of the merged Black Gangster Disciples Nation.

edges.evidence_quote: After Barksdale's death, ideological differences led to the creation of two rival distinct factions: the "Black Gangsters Disciples" and the "Black Disciples".
edges.context: Black Gangsters Disciples split from Black Disciples as a rival faction after Barksdale's death.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Gangster Disciples ( conflict ) , Vice Lords , Black P. Stones , Mickey Cobras
edges.context: Infobox lists Vice Lords as a rival.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Gangster Disciples ( conflict ) , Vice Lords , Black P. Stones , Mickey Cobras
edges.context: Infobox lists Black P. Stones as a rival.
